mnemonics: Going Downhill Rapidly Always Decreases Elevation (Gradient Descent)
keyPoints: The algorithm iteratively moves in the negative direction of the gradient to find a local minimum.
The learning rate is a crucial hyperparameter that controls the step size during each iteration.
It is guaranteed to converge to a local minimum for convex functions.
commonMistakes: Setting the learning rate too high, causing the algorithm to overshoot and diverge.
Applying the method to non-convex functions without awareness that it may get stuck in a local minimum.
